item.title: Dollar Falls Against Major Peers on Report of US-Iran Deal
item.description: The dollar fell against all of its major peers after a report that the US and Iran reached an agreement to extend a ceasefire, pending the approval of President Donald Trump.

item.title: Private Credit Snubs $2.5 Billion Deal by Thoma Bravo’s Sophos
item.description: Just last year, private credit firms were falling over themselves to lend to software borrowers like Sophos. Now, AI anxiety has snarled up the market so much that Thoma Bravo’s $2.5 billion refinancing for the cybersecurity firm is facing hesitant lenders.

item.title: Delfin Is Close to Proceeding With First US Floating LNG Project
item.description: Delfin Midstream Inc. is close to greenlighting what would be the first floating liquefied natural gas export site in the US, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Waymo to Deploy Robotaxi Built With Zeekr to Expand Public Rides
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Waymo unit will deploy a new autonomous vehicle designed specifically for robotaxi use, offering public rides in California and Arizona without human supervision.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Director Sounds Alarm on Inflation Outlook
item.description: Brazil central bank Monetary Policy Director Nilton David said rising 2028 inflation expectations are a growing concern for policymakers, highlighting fears that a global energy shock could pressure the long-term outlook.

item.title: Avis’ Shock Split With Vendor Revives Credit’s Big Software Fear
item.description: It in many ways embodies investors’ worst fears of how a SaaSpocalypse might play out.

item.title: Odd Lots Is Coming to Hong Kong
item.description: Come test your wits.

item.title: Gold Erases Losses After Report of US-Iran Truce Deal
item.description: Gold erased losses after Axios reported that the US and Iran are on the verge of a deal to extend the ceasefire, pending final approval from US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Drone Stocks Soar on News of Trump Team Eyeing Funding Deals
item.description: Shares of drone makers and suppliers rallied Thursday after a report said the Trump Administration is exploring potential funding deals with a group of companies to increase domestic production and lower costs.

item.title: EU’s Economic Powers Seek Unity to Push Capital Markets Merger
item.description: The European Union’s six largest economies are advancing plans for a bloc-wide capital markets union, hoping their collective voice will spur the continent to action after years of delay.

item.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $73,000 Despite Trump’s Crypto Capital Push
item.description: Bitcoin briefly fell below $73,000 despite a fresh push from Donald Trump for the CLARITY Act, suggesting pro-crypto messaging from Washington is losing its ability to drive gains in digital assets.

item.title: Fed’s Musalem Says Officials Can’t Rely on AI Boom to Fix Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem said policymakers cannot depend on a potential productivity boom from artificial intelligence to ease elevated inflation.

item.title: Canada’s Current Account Deficit Surged in First Quarter as Imports Rose
item.description: Canadians imported a record amount of goods in the first quarter, driven primarily by surging prices for gold and other metals, causing the current account deficit to widen.

item.title: Bank of Canada Says Markets More Vulnerable to Sharp Correction
item.description: The Bank of Canada said the financial system has functioned well through recent global shocks, but highlighted the risk of an asset price correction as well as vulnerabilities related to the role hedge funds are playing in debt markets.

item.title: Treasuries Rise After Reported US-Iran Deal to Extend Truce
item.description: Treasuries rose after a report that the US and Iran reached an agreement to extend a truce and work toward an end to a three-month war that’s ignited an oil-driven surge in inflation.

item.title: Oil Tankers Suffer Drone Attacks Off Turkey’s Black Sea Coast
item.description: Three crude-oil tankers sanctioned for being part of Russia’s shadow fleet were attacked by drones off Turkey’s Black Sea coast overnight, according to a local port agent.

item.title: Bridgepoint Raises €5 Billion for European Direct Lending Fund
item.description: Bridgepoint Group Plc is set to raise about €5 billion ($5.8 billion) to target financing opportunities in Europe’s direct lending market, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Space Startup Raises Funds for Laser Satellite Communications
item.description: Observable Space raised $90 million in its debut funding round as the company seeks to use lasers to help satellites and potentially orbiting data centers move information to and from Earth.

item.title: Actis Raises $2.5 Billion at Energy Fund’s First Close
item.description: Actis has raised $2.5 billion at first close for its flagship energy fund, putting it about 40% of the way to its goal, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: South Africa Hikes Rates, Hints at More Tightening to Come
item.description: South Africa’s central bank raised borrowing costs for the first time since 2023 and suggested more policy tightening may be needed if the Iran war drags on, as policymakers sought to contain higher inflation sparked by the conflict.

item.title: US Funding Markets Are Flooded With Cash That’s Here to Stay
item.description: An abundance of cash in US funding markets appears to be driven by deeper structural shifts that are unlocking billions of dollars in balance-sheet capacity at the biggest banks, Wall Street strategists say.

item.title: Argentine Provinces Tap Global Markets That Sovereign Avoids
item.description: Argentine provinces are tapping global bond markets at the fastest pace in nearly a decade, even as the federal government sticks to short-term debt in the local market amid investor fears over the 2027 elections.

item.title: Fed’s Williams Says Unclear How Productivity Boom Impacts Rates
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said it’s unclear how a lift in productivity will ultimately impact interest rates and the central bank’s policymaking.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Tick Up to 215,000, Highest in a Month
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits rose slightly, while remaining at a level consistent with a stable labor market.

item.title: US Consumer Spending Edges Up as Inflation Accelerates
item.description: US consumer spending edged up in April, with annual inflation accelerating to the highest level since 2023 as the Iran war drove up energy prices.

item.title: European Banks Could Cut 20% of Jobs on AI, Morgan Stanley Says
item.description: The rapid spread of artificial intelligence may enable European banks to reduce their headcount by as much as a fifth over the “shorter term,” according to Morgan Stanley analysts.

item.title: MetLife Clashes With Ares Over Struggling Football Investment
item.description: Two major financial firms have clashed over the restructuring of Eagle Football, the group that holds stakes in a number of football teams including the storied French club Olympique Lyonnais.

item.title: Apple’s iOS 27 Siri Overhaul and AI Features Previewed in New Images
item.description: The iPhone maker looks to stage a comeback in digital assistants and artificial intelligence.

item.title: BCI Expands Into Financing Private Equity Funds Amid Deal Slump
item.description: British Columbia Investment Management Corp. created a team within its private equity unit to provide financing to buyout firms that are increasingly looking for new ways to drum up cash amid a prolonged dealmaking drought.

item.title: Oura Ring 5 Is 40% Smaller, Detects Blood Pressure Changes and Sleep Disturbances
item.description: Oura Health Oy, the popular smart ring maker seeking to go public this year, unveiled a significantly thinner and lighter new model along with new wellness features.
